UF-108-1.label: Route D hidden-circularity
UF-108-1.description: Route D (numerical chart-map fitting) as scoped in 069r2 envelope §3 has hidden-circularity: numerical fitting (a_0, a_1, a_2) -> (alpha, beta) requires a working forward readout from P_III(D_6) numerical solutions to (alpha, beta) parameter values, which IS the chart-map Routes A/B/C are constructing symbolically. As scoped, Route D recovers a single point (V_quad's image) not a function.
UF-108-1.implication: Route D demotes from SOLO_PRIMARY_PARALLEL to VERIFICATION_PARALLEL_SECONDARY in any 069r3 cascade.
UF-108-1.action: 069r2-followup envelope §3 should reframe Route D scope; round-2 substrate-paste must include independent (alpha, beta) extraction operation spec.
UF-108-1.surfaced_by: Claude Opus 4.7 / Claude.ai web QD reasoning

UF-108-2.label: Route F surfacing pre-staged risk
UF-108-2.description: If FW math-ph/0201051 excerpts (when pasted in round-1 substrate-paste) reveal Sakai D_6 surface-type machinery as the actual chart-map mechanism (rather than sigma-form / Hamiltonian P_III), the entire A-E route framework is incomplete. Synth pre-committed to flagging ROUTE_FRAME_INCOMPLETE per HALT-S5 rather than silently folding into A-E.
UF-108-2.implication: 069r1's 102-disposition LOW-yield risk under surface-type machinery is not eliminated by 069r2 framework.
UF-108-2.action: Agent vocabulary watch-list during FW excerpt absorption: Sakai D_6 surface, surface-type machinery, Mukai pair, rational surface, blow-up, exceptional curve, W(D_6) extended affine Weyl. If any triggers fire, halt absorption and surface as Route F surfacing.
UF-108-2.surfaced_by: Claude Opus 4.7 / Claude.ai web QF caveat 4
